seo.title: Tri-Cities WA Photographer | It's A Keeper Photography
seo.description: Warm, natural portraits for seniors, families, newborns and businesses in Richland, Kennewick and Pasco. 20+ years behind the camera. 96 five-star reviews.

hero.imageAlt: A couple sharing a quiet embrace beneath autumn trees during a golden-hour session in Richland, WA
hero.heading: Tri-Cities Photographer for the Seasons Worth Keeping
hero.subhead: Warm, natural portraits for seniors, families, newborns and businesses across Richland, Kennewick and Pasco — photographed in golden light, guided so you never have to wonder what to do with your hands.
hero.trustBar: 20+ years behind the camera · 96 five-star Google reviews · Richland, WA — serving all of the Tri-Cities

why.paragraph: I'll guide you when you need it, then leave room for the laugh, the hand squeeze, and the look that happens on its own. We'll choose a Tri-Cities location that feels like you and follow the light together.
why.reassurance: You never have to worry about knowing how to pose — that's my job. I'll help you plan what to wear ahead of time, and during the session I'll give you simple prompts that help you relax and just be yourselves. Most people tell me the same thing afterward: "That was actually fun."
why.rearImageAlt: A high school senior standing among warm autumn leaves in the Tri-Cities
why.frontImageAlt: A couple holding their baby close in golden autumn light near Richland

sessions.heading: Choose the Story You're In
sessions.intro: Every season deserves to be remembered. These are the ones I photograph best.

cards.label: Seniors
cards.imageAlt: High school senior in a black dress photographed at golden hour in Richland
cards.description: Your senior year only happens once. Relaxed, confident senior pictures that actually look like you — not a pose from someone else's Pinterest board.

cards.label: Families
cards.imageAlt: A family laughing together during an outdoor Tri-Cities portrait session
cards.description: The tiny hands, the real laughs, the way everyone piles together — golden-hour family sessions that feel like play, not like standing still.

cards.label: Newborns
cards.imageAlt: A mother kissing her newborn during a gentle in-home newborn session in Richland
cards.description: Those first days pass so quickly. Gentle, unhurried newborn sessions that preserve just how small they started.

cards.label: Branding
cards.imageAlt: Tri-Cities business owner photographed during a custom branding session
cards.description: Custom business photography for Tri-Cities entrepreneurs — images that show the person and heart behind your work.

cards.label: Headshots
cards.imageAlt: Professional headshot of a Tri-Cities professional in natural light
cards.description: A headshot you're proud to put on LinkedIn, your website, and everywhere your name shows up.

photoBreak.imageAlt: Parents holding their young child in glowing Columbia Basin evening light
photoBreak.scriptPhrase: the light moves; the feeling stays
photoBreak.srHeading: Golden-Hour Photography in Richland, Kennewick & Pasco
photoBreak.heading: Golden-Hour Photography in Richland, Kennewick & Pasco
photoBreak.paragraph: I've called the Tri-Cities home since 2005, and after twenty years of chasing evening light here, I know where it lands — the river paths, the orchards, the open fields, and the quiet corners most people drive past. Whether we meet in Richland, Kennewick or Pasco, I'll bring you somewhere the light — and your family — can do what they do best.
photoBreak.buttonLabel: Best places to take pictures in the Tri-Cities

meetLisa.story: My journey into photography began with my own children, not a business plan. When they were little, I wanted more than rushed studio portraits — I wanted photographs that captured who they truly were. What started with my own kids has become the privilege of documenting life's most meaningful moments for hundreds of Tri-Cities families, seniors and couples over the past 20 years.
meetLisa.credit: I photographed my own grandparents once — laughing, holding hands, sharing the quiet love they'd built over a lifetime. I had no idea how precious those photographs would become after my grandpa passed away. That's the truth I carry into every session: I'm not just taking photographs for today. I'm preserving moments that may one day be a family's most treasured memories.
meetLisa.portraitAlt: Lisa, owner of It’s A Keeper Photography, holding her camera in the Tri-Cities

howItWorks.heading: How a Session Works
howItWorks.intro: I reserve most sessions four to six weeks ahead so we have time to plan it right.

steps.title: You reach out
steps.sentence: Tell me who you want to photograph and what this season means to you.

steps.title: We talk
steps.sentence: We'll plan everything by phone — wardrobe guidance, location ideas, and the little details that make it feel like you.

steps.title: Golden hour
steps.sentence: We meet outdoors, move gently, and let natural connection do most of the work. You'll probably hear me say "get closer… closer!" more than once.

steps.title: Your gallery
steps.sentence: I edit each frame in my warm, true-to-you style and send your story home.

howItWorks.buttonLabel: The full experience, start to finish

items.question: What areas do you serve?
items.answer: I'm based in Richland, Washington, and photograph throughout the Tri-Cities — Richland, Kennewick, Pasco and the surrounding areas. Most sessions happen outdoors at golden hour, at a location we choose together.

items.question: How far in advance should I book?
items.answer: I reserve most sessions four to six weeks ahead. Senior sessions and fall family sessions fill fastest, so if you have a season in mind, reach out early and we'll plan it right.

items.question: What if we're awkward in front of the camera?
items.answer: Then you're my favorite kind of client. Most people who step in front of my camera feel a little nervous at first — my whole approach is built around gentle prompts and guidance, so you always know what to do. You'll leave saying it was actually fun. Promise.

items.question: Do you photograph weddings?
items.answer: I photograph small, intimate elopements only — not full wedding days or events. If you're planning something small and meaningful, I'd love to hear about it.

items.question: What should we wear?
items.answer: That's something we figure out together before your session. I'll help you choose outfits that photograph beautifully and that you can actually move, sit and play in comfortably.

inquiry.heading: Let’s plan your golden hour
inquiry.scriptLine: the light is already on its way...
inquiry.supportLine: This takes less than a minute. I read and answer every inquiry myself.
inquiry.backgroundImageAlt: A senior standing in a sunlit Tri-Cities field near the end of the day
